title: malva rotundifolia subsp. parviflora
intro: Malva rotundifolia subsp. parviflora, also known as the small-flowered mallow or round-leaved mallow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Malva rotundiflora subsp. parviflora include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
